accepted.assertion:
Erdős Problem #793: declared status 'open'. Formalized: no. Let $F(n)$ be the maximum possible size of a subset $A\subseteq\{1,\ldots,n\}$ such that $a
mid bc$ whenever $a,b,c\in A$ with $a
eq b$ and $a
eq c$. Is there a constant $C$ such that\[F(n)=\pi(n)+(C+o(1))n^{2/3}(\log n)^{-2}?\] Current best: Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er38} proved there exist constants $0<c_1\leq c_2$ such that\[\pi(n)+c_1n^{2/3}(\log n)^{-2}\leq F(n) \leq \pi(n)+c_2n^{2/3}(\log n)^{-2}.\]Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er69} gave a simple proof that $F(n) \leq \pi(n)+n^{2/3}$: define a graph with vertex set the union of those integers in $[1,n^{2/3}]$ with all primes $p\in (n^{2/3},n]$. It is easy to see that every $m\leq n$ can be written as $uv$ where $u\leq n^{2/3}$ and $v$ is either prime or $\leq n^{2/3}$, and hence there are $\geq \lvert Avert$ many edges. This can be improved to give the upper bound mentioned by using a subset of integers in $[1,n^{2/3}]$. More generally, one can ask for such an asymptotic for the size of sets such that no $a\in A$ divides the product of $r$ distinct other elements of $A$, with the exponent $2/3$ replaced by $ rac{2}{r+1}$. Prize: no. Tags: number theory.
